agitation.note: First 30s continuous, then first 10s of every minute

notes: Official ADOX FX-39 II datasheet (29 Jan 2018). Kodak T-Max 100 in FX-39 1+9; mean gradient 0.65 (Rev. 1/2018). Same emulsion as Kodak T-Max 100, sold as Ektapan 100 since 2026; times per the T-Max 100 datasheet.

agitation.note: First 30s continuous, then first 10s of every minute

notes: Official ADOX FX-39 II datasheet (29 Jan 2018). Kodak T-Max 400 in FX-39 1+9; mean gradient 0.65 (Rev. 1/2018). Same emulsion as Kodak T-Max 400, sold as Ektapan 400 since 2026; times per the T-Max 400 datasheet.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain film with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE the label processing time to clear pink/purple dyes. Times shown are 2x the base Df96 scheme. Same emulsion as Kodak T-Max 100, sold as Ektapan 100 since 2026; times per the T-Max 100 datasheet.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE processing time (2x base Df96 scheme) to clear dyes. Same emulsion as Kodak T-Max 400, sold as Ektapan 400 since 2026; times per the T-Max 400 datasheet.

agitation.note: Intermittent agitation at 24C/75F; time is minimum (2x base for tabular dye clearing).

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). High-speed tabular film processed at native EI 1600 per label; requires double processing time to clear dyes. Can be pushed to 3200 by adding 10F/6C. Same emulsion as Kodak T-Max P3200, sold as Ektapan P3200 since 2026; times per the T-Max P3200 datasheet.
